name: Cabo Verde

visa_free.notes: Acordo no âmbito da CPLP. Listed on the official gov.cv page as 180 days. Ordinary passport: 90 days; up to 180 days with official document proving official service mission or business travel (Lei n.º 18/II/82, Decretos n.º 7/95, 106/85 e 24/97). Diplomatic and Service passports: 180 days.
Visa-free entry; 90-day limit (ECOWAS member). Listed as 'Costa do Marfim' on the Portuguese version of the official page.
Protocolo de Livre Circulação de Pessoas (ECOWAS) and Acordo no âmbito da CPLP. 180 days for diplomatic/service/ordinary passports.
Visa-free entry; 30-day limit. Listed as 'Holanda' on the Portuguese version of the official page.
Visa-free entry; 30-day limit. Listed as 'Hong Kong' (SAR) on the official page.
Acordo no âmbito da CPLP. Ordinary passport: up to 90 days; up to 180 days with official document proving service mission. Diplomatic, Service and Special passports: 180 days.

Ordinary passport: 30 days per Resolução n.º 134/2018. Diplomatic, Special and Service passports: indefinite stay (Acordo Bilateral + CPLP). The official MNEC PDF states 'Indeterminado' (indefinite) for Diplomatic/Special/Service passport holders.

Visa-free entry; 90 days. Listed as 'Rússia' on the official gov.cv visa-exempt page. Ordinary passport holders are visa-free. Based on the bilateral agreement Decreto n.º 8/95, de 28 de agosto (originally diplomatic/service passports); the gov.cv page confirms ordinary passport holders are now included in the exemption.
Acordo no âmbito da CPLP. Ordinary passport: 90 days; up to 180 days with official document proving service mission. Diplomatic, Service and Special passports: 180 days.
Visa-free entry; 30-day limit. Listed as 'Vaticano' on the official page.

visa_on_arrival.nationality: Nationals of countries without Cabo Verde diplomatic representation
visa_on_arrival.notes: Citizens residing in countries where Cabo Verde has no diplomatic representation may apply for visas upon entry at the borders per portalconsular.mnec.gov.cv. Note: as of January 1, 2026, Cabo Verde requires prior entry visas for 96 nationalities (see EASE official notice: ease.gov.cv/assets/lista_de_paises.pdf). This border-on-arrival exception applies only to those without access to a Cabo Verde consulate.

e_visa.notes: Cabo Verde operates an online pre-arrival visa application system via portalconsular.mnec.gov.cv. Tourist visa allows up to 90 days stay (extendable once for additional 90 days). All travelers (including visa-free) must also pre-register via ease.gov.cv and pay the Airport Security Fee (TSA, approximately EUR 30.83 / CVE 3,400 for international flights) prior to arrival.

visa_policy.notes: The official gov.cv page (paises-isentos-de-visto-para-cabo-verde) lists 59 visa-exempt nationalities with specific stay limits, confirmed by the official MNEC consular portal PDF (paisesisentosvistos.pdf). All foreign travelers must pre-register via ease.gov.cv up to 5 days before travel and pay an Airport Security Fee (TSA: CVE 3,400 / approx EUR 30.83 for international flights; CVE 150 for inter-island). As of January 1, 2026, Cabo Verde requires prior visas for 91 nationalities per the corrected Despacho n.º 244/GMAI/2026 (published in the Official Bulletin on January 23, 2026), which corrected and replaced the original November 2025 version (Despacho n.º 244/GMAI/2025 - BOE n.º 223, II Série de 24-11-2025). The original November 2025 dispatch listed 96 countries; the January 2026 correction removed 5 - Cameroon, India, Rwanda, Taiwan, and Turkey - reducing the total to 91. The corrected 91-country prior-visa-required list (per ease.gov.cv/assets/lista_de_paises.pdf as cited by the corrective dispatch and Expresso das Ilhas coverage of 15 Feb 2026) includes: Afghanistan, Algeria, Armenia, Azerbaijan, Bahrain, Bangladesh, Belarus, Bolivia, Botswana, Brunei, Burundi, Bhutan, Cambodia, Chad, Chile, Colombia, Comoros, Congo, North Korea, Costa Rica, Djibouti, Dominica, Ecuador, Egypt, El Salvador, Eritrea, Eswatini, Ethiopia, Philippines, Gabon, Guatemala, Guyana, Haiti, Honduras, Indonesia, Iran, Iraq, Jamaica, Jordan, Kazakhstan, Kiribati, Laos, Lesotho, Lebanon, Libya, Madagascar, Malawi, Mauritania, Mexico, Mongolia, Myanmar, Namibia, Nauru, Nepal, Nicaragua, Oman, Palestine, Palau, Panama, Papua New Guinea, Pakistan, Paraguay, Peru, Puerto Rico, Kyrgyzstan, Central African Republic, Democratic Republic of Congo, Dominican Republic, Samoa, Syria, Somalia, Sri Lanka, Sudan, South Sudan, Suriname, Tajikistan, Tanzania, Tonga, Tokelau, Trinidad and Tobago, Tunisia, Turkmenistan, Tuvalu, Uganda, Uzbekistan, Vanuatu, Venezuela, Vietnam, Yemen, Zambia, Zimbabwe (91 total). Cameroon, India, Rwanda, Taiwan, and Turkey are NOT on the corrected prior-visa-required list and are therefore subject to standard visa processing (e-visa/consular) rather than mandatory prior consular appointment. The official list does not include USA, Canada, Japan, Australia, or several other countries that some third-party sources indicate may enter visa-free; these could not be confirmed on the official gov.cv page. Cuba is listed on the official gov.cv page as 'durante a missão' (during the mission) which refers to the bilateral diplomatic/official mission arrangement (Decreto n.º 79/82) and applies to diplomatic/service/official passport holders only - not ordinary tourists.

conditional_access.conditions: Nationals of countries on the prior-visa-required list (91 countries per corrected Despacho n.º 244/GMAI/2026, published 23 Jan 2026, correcting the original 96-country Despacho n.º 244/GMAI/2025 of Nov 2025) are exempt from the prior visa requirement if they hold a valid residence authorization (autorização de residência válida) issued by an EU Member State, Iceland, Liechtenstein, San Marino, Switzerland, or Vatican. Verbatim: 'titulares de autorizações de residência válidas emitidas por Estados-Membros da União Europeia, Islândia, Liechtenstein, Reino Unido, San Marino, Suíça e Vaticano'. No max stay stated in the exemption clause; traveller enters without needing a prior visa.

Nationals of countries on the prior-visa-required list (91 countries per corrected Despacho n.º 244/GMAI/2026, published 23 Jan 2026) are exempt from the prior visa requirement if they hold a valid residence authorization (autorização de residência válida) issued by the United Kingdom. Verbatim Portuguese: 'titulares de autorizações de residência válidas emitidas por ... Reino Unido'. No max stay stated in the exemption clause.

credential.subtype: any valid residence document guaranteeing unconditional readmission

conditional_access.conditions: Nationals of countries on the prior-visa-required list (91 countries per corrected Despacho n.º 244/GMAI/2026, published 23 Jan 2026) are exempt from the prior visa requirement if they are legal residents in the United States, provided the document guarantees unconditional readmission. Verbatim: 'residentes legais no Brasil, Canadá e Estados Unidos da América, desde que o documento garanta a readmissão incondicional do titular nesses países'. This covers US permanent residents (Green Card holders) and other legal resident categories. No max stay stated in the exemption clause.

credential.subtype: any valid residence document guaranteeing unconditional readmission

conditional_access.conditions: Nationals of countries on the prior-visa-required list (91 countries per corrected Despacho n.º 244/GMAI/2026, published 23 Jan 2026) are exempt from the prior visa requirement if they are legal residents in Canada, provided the document guarantees unconditional readmission. Verbatim: 'residentes legais no Brasil, Canadá e Estados Unidos da América, desde que o documento garanta a readmissão incondicional do titular nesses países'. No max stay stated in the exemption clause.

credential.subtype: any valid residence document guaranteeing unconditional readmission

conditional_access.conditions: Nationals of countries on the prior-visa-required list (91 countries per corrected Despacho n.º 244/GMAI/2026, published 23 Jan 2026) are exempt from the prior visa requirement if they are legal residents in Brazil, provided the document guarantees unconditional readmission. Verbatim: 'residentes legais no Brasil, Canadá e Estados Unidos da América, desde que o documento garanta a readmissão incondicional do titular nesses países'. No max stay stated in the exemption clause.

Holders of diplomatic, official, or service passports of any country are exempt from the prior visa requirement and may receive diplomatic, official, or courtesy visas. Despacho 244/GMAI/2025 states: holders of diplomatic or service passports, as well as citizens traveling by invitation of Cabo Verde authorities, receive differentiated treatment (diplomatic, official, or courtesy visas). Pre-existing bilateral agreements: Cuba - Decreto n.º 79/82, de 28 de agosto (diplomatic/service/official passports, duration of mission); Russia - Decreto n.º 8/95, de 28 de agosto (diplomatic and service passports, 90 days, though Russia ordinary passport holders are now also visa-free per the gov.cv exempt list).

Decreto n.º 79/82, de 28 de agosto. Diplomatic, Service and Official passports only; duration is 'during the mission' (durante a missão). The gov.cv visa-exempt page lists Cuba as 'durante a missão' (during the mission) - this refers to the bilateral diplomatic/official mission arrangement, not ordinary tourist access. Ordinary passport holders from Cuba require a visa.

cbi.notes: No citizenship-by-investment program was found on any official Cabo Verde government website (gov.cv, mnec.gov.cv, e-portaldef.gov.cv, mf.gov.cv). The Green Card is a permanent residence permit for real estate investors, not a citizenship program. Third-party/non-official sources mention investment-linked routes to citizenship but these were not confirmed on official government pages.

programs.name: Green Card - Autorização de Residência Permanente (Real Estate Investment)
programs.notes: A permanent residence permit granted to foreigners who purchase built or under-construction real estate worth at least EUR 80,000 (CVE 8,821,200) in municipalities with per-capita GDP below the national average, OR at least EUR 120,000 (CVE 13,231,800) in municipalities with per-capita GDP at or above the national average. Investment must be paid with funds transferred from abroad. Authorized by Lei nº 30/IX/2018 (creates the differentiated status for second-residence holders via Green Card) and Decreto-Regulamentar nº 1/2020 (establishes the implementing regulations). Application portal at e-portaldef.gov.cv. The Green Card grants permanent residence directly. Both the law and regulation are listed on the AAI (Alta Autoridade para a Imigração) official documentation portal at aai.gov.cv.

programs.name: Autorização de Residência - Actividade de Investimento ou Actividade Económica Relevante
programs.notes: Temporary Residence Permit for foreigners carrying out investment activity or relevant economic activity in Cabo Verde. No minimum investment amount is stated on the official page. Requires valid entry visa, criminal record certificate, medical certificate, international vaccination certificate, Social Security registration, proof of accommodation, and documentation of the investment activity (authorization/registration by competent authority, External Investor Certificate, or proof of company employing at least 50% Cabo Verdean workers, or proof of activity of recognized national interest).

programs.name: Visto Nómadas Digitais (Digital Nomad Visa)
programs.eligibility: Persons working remotely for a company or entity outside Cabo Verde. Required documents: passport, criminal record from country of origin, proof of employment/contract with remote employer, proof of income, proof of accommodation in Cabo Verde, medical certificate.
programs.notes: Listed on the official National Police / e-Residencia portal (e-portaldef.gov.cv) under Visa types. The application portal was listed as 'Em manutenção' (under maintenance) at the time of research (June 2026). No duration or income threshold stated on the official page.

visa_types.name: Visto de Trânsito (Transit Visa)
visa_types.purpose: For travellers transiting through Cabo Verde to another country who disembark and remain in the country briefly before continuing their journey.
visa_types.notes: Stay limited to 4 days; extendable once. Available via the consular portal at portalconsular.mnec.gov.cv. Transit passengers remaining airside (without clearing immigration) do not need a transit visa.

visa_types.name: Visto Diplomático, Oficial ou de Cortesia (Diplomatic / Official / Courtesy Visa)
visa_types.purpose: Issued to holders of diplomatic, official, or service passports, and to persons traveling at the invitation of Cabo Verdean authorities.
visa_types.notes: Must be used within 90 days of issuance. Allows stays of up to 30 days per entry with multiple entries possible during the 90-day validity window. Holders of diplomatic and service passports of any country are generally exempt from prior visa requirements per Despacho n.º 244/GMAI/2025.

visa_types.name: Visto de Turismo (Tourist Visa)
visa_types.purpose: For recreational activities, leisure, and cruise travel to Cabo Verde.
visa_types.notes: Must be used within 60 days of issuance. Allows a stay of up to 90 days, extendable once for an additional 90 days. Application via portalconsular.mnec.gov.cv. All travellers must also pre-register via ease.gov.cv and pay the Airport Security Fee (TSA: approx CVE 3,400 / EUR 30.83 for international arrivals).

visa_types.name: Visto Temporário - Missão Empresarial (Temporary Visa: Business Mission)
visa_types.purpose: For business missions, short-term commercial activities, and professional engagements in Cabo Verde.
visa_types.notes: Sub-category of the Visto Temporário. Single-entry variant permits stays up to 180 days. Multiple-entry variant allows a total of 90 days within one year. Also covers short-term professional/technical activities. Application via portalconsular.mnec.gov.cv.

visa_types.name: Visto Temporário - Cultural (Temporary Visa: Cultural)
visa_types.purpose: For cultural trips, artistic residencies, and participation in cultural events in Cabo Verde.
visa_types.notes: Sub-category of the Visto Temporário. Must be used within 180 days of issuance. Multiple-entry variant allows 90 days total over one year. Application via portalconsular.mnec.gov.cv.

visa_types.name: Visto Temporário - Tratamento Médico (Temporary Visa: Medical Treatment)
visa_types.purpose: For foreign nationals travelling to Cabo Verde to receive medical treatment.
visa_types.notes: Sub-category of the Visto Temporário. Must be used within 180 days of issuance. Application via portalconsular.mnec.gov.cv.

visa_types.name: Visto Temporário - Visita Familiar (Temporary Visa: Family Visit)
visa_types.purpose: For foreign nationals visiting family members residing in Cabo Verde on a short-term basis.
visa_types.notes: Sub-category of the Visto Temporário. Multiple-entry variant allows 90 days total within one year. Application via portalconsular.mnec.gov.cv.

visa_types.name: Visto de Residência - Trabalho (Residence Visa: Employment)
visa_types.purpose: For foreign nationals intending to take up professional employment and establish permanent residence in Cabo Verde.
visa_types.notes: Sub-category of the Visto de Residência. Allows the holder to remain for 6 months, extendable until a final decision is made on the residence permit application. Must obtain a full Autorização de Residência after arrival. Application via portalconsular.mnec.gov.cv.

visa_types.name: Visto de Residência - Investimento (Residence Visa: Investment)
visa_types.purpose: For foreign nationals intending to carry out investment or economically relevant activities and establish residence in Cabo Verde.
visa_types.notes: Sub-category of the Visto de Residência. Grants 6-month stay extendable pending residence permit decision. Related to the Green Card (real estate investment, min EUR 80,000–120,000) and Autorização de Residência for investment activity. Application via portalconsular.mnec.gov.cv and e-portaldef.gov.cv.

visa_types.name: Visto de Residência - Estudante (Residence Visa: Studies)
visa_types.purpose: For foreign nationals enrolling in higher education or courses lasting more than one year in Cabo Verde.
visa_types.notes: Sub-category of the Visto de Residência. Applies to study programs lasting over one year. Grants a 6-month stay extendable pending residence permit decision. Application via portalconsular.mnec.gov.cv.

visa_types.name: Visto de Residência - Reagrupamento Familiar (Residence Visa: Family Reunification)
visa_types.purpose: For foreign nationals joining family members who are legally resident in Cabo Verde.
visa_types.notes: Sub-category of the Visto de Residência for permanent family reunification. Grants a 6-month stay extendable pending residence permit decision. Application via portalconsular.mnec.gov.cv.

visa_types.name: Visto Nómadas Digitais (Digital Nomad Visa)
visa_types.purpose: For persons who work remotely for a company or entity located outside Cabo Verde and wish to reside in the country.
visa_types.notes: Listed on the official National Police / e-Residência portal (e-portaldef.gov.cv). Required documents include: passport, criminal record from country of origin, proof of remote employment/contract with employer outside Cabo Verde, proof of income, proof of accommodation in Cabo Verde, and a medical certificate. The application portal was listed as 'Em manutenção' (under maintenance) as of June 2026. No income threshold, duration of stay, or fee stated on the official page.

outbound_official.note: No official Cabo Verde government outbound visa-list page located. The official consular portal FAQ (portalconsular.mnec.gov.cv/en/perguntas-frequentes) explicitly states 'Os portadores do passaporte cabo-verdiano podem entrar sem visto, ou requerer visto à entrada, nos países constantes na lista Passport Index' and redirects citizens to passportindex.org - an aggregator - rather than maintaining its own government-published list of destinations where Cabo Verde ordinary-passport holders may travel without a prior consular visa. The MNEC consular portal and gov.cv portals focus exclusively on inbound travel to Cabo Verde. Rely on destination-side data.

agreements: CPLP (Community of Portuguese Language Countries) special mobility agreement

validation_notes: 2026-06-29 RE-VERIFICATION: Re-fetched gov.cv/paises-isentos-de-visto-para-cabo-verde (Portuguese) and gov.cv/en/paises-isentos-de-visto-para-cabo-verde (English) - both confirm 59 visa-free nationalities unchanged (same list, same stay durations). portalconsular.mnec.gov.cv/en/vistos EASE pre-registration requirement and TSA fee confirmed still in effect. ease.gov.cv confirms January 2026 prior-visa requirement remains active. boe.incv.cv Despacho 244/GMAI/2025 loaded (HTML title confirmed) - full text not extractable this run. CORRECTION APPLIED THIS RUN: prior-visa-required country count in notes and conditional_access corrected from '96' to '91', reflecting the January 23, 2026 corrective dispatch (Despacho n.º 244/GMAI/2026) which reduced the list by removing Cameroon, India, Rwanda, Taiwan, and Turkey. Sources reached this run (official pages that loaded with usable content): 5 (gov.cv x2, portalconsular.mnec.gov.cv, ease.gov.cv, boe.incv.cv title-only).

2026-06-29 CORRECTION: Prior-visa-required country count corrected from '96' to '91'. The original November 2025 dispatch (Despacho n.º 244/GMAI/2025, BOE n.º 223, II Série de 24-11-2025) listed 96 countries. A corrective dispatch (Despacho n.º 244/GMAI/2026) was published on January 23, 2026 in the Official Bulletin, removing 5 countries - Cameroon, India, Rwanda, Taiwan, and Turkey - from the mandatory prior-visa-required list, reducing the total to 91. Source: Expresso das Ilhas (Cabo Verdean newspaper) article of 15 Feb 2026 citing the corrective dispatch (expressodasilhas.cv/pais/2026/02/15/novo-despacho-redefine-paises-sujeitos-a-visto-e-reforca-controlo-fronteirico/101399). The previous validation note from 2026-06-27 that 'corrected' the count from 91 to 96 was itself erroneous - it cited the pre-correction November 2025 version. The current correct count is 91.

2026-06-29 CORRECTION: The previous validation note (2026-06-27) stating 'India is explicitly named in the 96-country prior-visa-required list' is now superseded. India (IND) was REMOVED from the mandatory prior-visa-required list in the January 2026 corrective dispatch. India is therefore NOT subject to the mandatory prior consular visa requirement; Indian nationals may use standard visa channels (e-visa/consular application) rather than requiring a pre-travel in-person appointment. India remains NOT visa-free and does NOT appear in visa_policy.visa_free. The appropriate channel for Indian nationals is visa_policy.e_visa (standard consular/online visa processing). This record does not move India to visa_free - only to the standard (non-prior-mandatory) visa-required category.

2026-06-27 RE-VERIFICATION (prior run, preserved): Full re-fetch of primary official sources gov.cv/paises-isentos-de-visto-para-cabo-verde (Portuguese) and gov.cv/en/paises-isentos-de-visto-para-cabo-verde (English) confirms 59 visa-free countries unchanged. All nationality entries, stay durations (30/90/180 days), and Cuba 'durante a missão' classification confirmed against live pages. portalconsular.mnec.gov.cv/en/vistos pre-arrival EASE registration requirement and TSA fee still in effect. ease.gov.cv confirms January 2026 prior-visa requirement remains active. travel.gov.cv loaded but contained only COVID-era health info (no visa data). aai.gov.cv account-suspended and inaccessible.

CORRECTION (prior run): Russia (RUS) added to visa_policy.visa_free (90 days, ordinary passport). The official gov.cv page explicitly lists 'Rússia - 90 dias' among the 59 visa-exempt nationalities for ordinary passport holders.

CORRECTION (prior run): Source URL updated from 'https://www.gov.cv/en/paises-isentos-de-visto-para-cabo-verde' to 'https://www.gov.cv/paises-isentos-de-visto-para-cabo-verde' (Portuguese version is the primary official page).

CLARIFICATION: Cuba's 'durante a missão' listing on the gov.cv visa-exempt page applies only to diplomatic/service/official passport holders per Decreto n.º 79/82. Cuba ordinary passport holders require a visa. Cuba remains correctly in conditional_access only.
VERIFIED UNCHANGED: Angola (180 days), ECOWAS members (90 days), EU members (30 days) - all confirmed on official gov.cv page.
VERIFIED UNCHANGED: EASE pre-registration requirement and TSA fee (CVE 3,400 / approx EUR 30.83) confirmed still in effect as of June 2026.
VERIFIED UNCHANGED: Green Card RBI program (EUR 80,000 minimum) and Digital Nomad Visa remain on official portals.
VERIFIED UNCHANGED: cbi.has_program = false confirmed - no citizenship-by-investment program found on official pages.
gaps: The official gov.cv visa-exempt list (59 entries) does not include USA, Canada, Japan, Australia, New Zealand, Israel, Morocco, Macao, or Timor-Leste - these are not confirmed on any official Cabo Verde government page despite some third-party sources claiming visa-free access.

Russia: The exact legal basis for extending the existing Decreto 8/95 exemption (originally diplomatic/service only) to ordinary passport holders is not confirmed from the official gov.cv PDF (binary-inaccessible). The gov.cv page itself is the primary official confirmation of ordinary passport visa-free access.

Green Card RBI: Path to citizenship (if any) from Green Card permanent residence not stated on official page. Standard naturalization under Lei nº 66/VIII/2014 would apply but conditions not confirmed.
Green Card RBI: Exact processing time not stated on official page.
Investor residence permit (Actividade de Investimento): No minimum investment amount published on official page.
Digital Nomad visa: Income threshold, duration of stay, and processing time not stated on official page. Application portal listed as under maintenance at time of research (June 2026).
No official data found on fast-track programs for startups or highly skilled workers beyond the digital nomad visa.

Cuba visa exemption: Ordinary passport holders' exact requirements (visa-on-arrival vs. prior visa) not confirmed from official Cabo Verde government pages; third-party sources indicate visa-on-arrival. The 91-country prior-visa list (corrected Jan 2026) does not include Cuba, suggesting Cuba ordinary passport holders may still get visa-on-arrival, but this is unconfirmed from official sources.

Conditional access - credential type precision: Despacho 244/GMAI/2025/2026 uses the term 'residentes legais' (legal residents) for Brazil/Canada/US, and 'autorização de residência válida' (valid residence authorization) for EU/Iceland/Liechtenstein/UK/San Marino/Switzerland/Vatican. The decree does not specify individual permit subtypes. Full text could not be fetched directly from boe.incv.cv (PDF binary).
No OCI (Overseas Citizen of India) cardholder exemption found on any official Cabo Verde source.

Diplomatic/service passport blanket waiver: max stay not confirmed per Despacho 244/GMAI/2025/2026 text. Bilateral decrees for Cuba (Decreto 79/82) and Russia (Decreto 8/95) documented in the MNEC PDF.

Cameroon, India, Rwanda, Taiwan, Turkey: status after January 2026 removal from prior-visa-required list - the corrective dispatch confirms these 5 countries are no longer subject to the mandatory prior consular visa, but their precise visa category (e-visa eligible vs. standard consular application) was not confirmed from a directly loadable official Cabo Verde government page this run. These nationalities are NOT visa-free and are NOT moved to visa_policy.visa_free.

sources_checked: https://travel.gov.cv/ [checked 2026-06-27 - COVID-era content only, no visa data]

https://portalconsular.mnec.gov.cv/en/vistos [re-verified 2026-06-29 - EASE pre-registration and TSA fee confirmed]
